summary: Compact HN Product Scout with concrete recommendations and alternatives.
telegram_footer: 9 items surfaced | 25/25 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated

items.name: Mac mini M4
items.source_story_title: Qwen 3.6 27B is the sweet spot for local development
items.summary: Apple's compact desktop recommended as a quiet local LLM box.
items.why_included: A commenter explicitly recommends a Mac mini M4 with 64GB RAM to run Qwen 3.6 locally away from the desk.
items.evidence: One commenter says to use a Mac mini M4 with 64GB RAM, keep it in the basement or a few meters away, and connect over LAN or Tailscale for Qwen3.6 27B/35B.

items.name: MobaXterm
items.source_story_title: A native graphical shell for SSH
items.summary: SSH client with a file manager and X11 support.
items.why_included: Commenters point to it as an existing SSH GUI shell that already has the requested features.
items.evidence: One commenter says MobaXterm already does this, with a file manager on the side and X11 support.

items.name: DuckDNS
items.source_story_title: .self: A new top-level domain designed to support self-hosting
items.summary: Dynamic DNS service commenters said works fine for self-hosting.
items.why_included: A commenter recommends it as a practical hostname option in the self-hosting discussion.
items.evidence: A comment says a <something>.duckdns.org address works fine, framing DuckDNS as a simple self-hosting solution.

items.name: Dafny
items.source_story_title: You Don't Know Jack About Formal Verification
items.summary: Verification language used in class for a pseudo digital wallet exercise.
items.why_included: Commenters mention it as a hands-on formal-verification tool people have actually used.
items.evidence: One commenter says they had fun in a college class that used Dafny to build a pseudo digital wallet.

items.name: Frama-C
items.source_story_title: You Don't Know Jack About Formal Verification
items.summary: C analysis framework for proving properties and avoiding undefined behavior.
items.why_included: It gets cited as a useful way to reason about real C programs before adding new specs.
items.evidence: A commenter says loading arbitrary C into Frama-C gives you tons of properties to prove and helps guarantee termination and no undefined behavior.

items.name: Mullvad
items.source_story_title: The CEO of Mullvad is the main financer of the Swedish Örebro party
items.summary: Privacy VPN people said they were moving to after the controversy.
items.why_included: The thread repeatedly treats Mullvad as the privacy-first alternative people want to keep funding.
items.evidence: Commenters say they use Mullvad because it blocks logging, call it excellent, and discuss moving their money there instead of other VPNs.

items.name: Proton VPN
items.source_story_title: The CEO of Mullvad is the main financer of the Swedish Örebro party
items.summary: Privacy VPN named as the other big alternative in the Mullvad thread.
items.why_included: People compare Mullvad directly against Proton VPN while deciding where to move their money.
items.evidence: A commenter says they are happy with Proton, another says ProtonVPN is the comparison point, and others ask for a good VPN that is neither Mullvad nor Proton.

items.name: Bandcamp
items.summary: Music marketplace people preferred over Spotify for buying music directly.
items.why_included: The thread turns up a concrete alternative for listeners who want to support musicians instead of streaming slop.
items.evidence: One commenter says they are done with Spotify and would rather spend money on Bandcamp; another says you can just buy music directly there.

items.name: Nebula
items.summary: Curated video platform commenters compared favorably with YouTube.
items.why_included: It surfaces as a smaller, less-slop alternative in a media thread that otherwise complains about platform noise.
items.evidence: A commenter says they have similar feelings about Nebula vs YouTube, calling Nebula a smaller, more selective option.
